meta.setting: Back rooms, claim offices, courthouse counters, mine ledgers, and private roads around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with ownership pressure, debt, law heat, company power, and hard choices
meta.issue_title: Page: Claim Kings and Combine Men

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Gold rush mining communities, claims, road pressure, settlement, and resource conflict.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, courthouse records, and old town material.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Claim Kings and Combine Men cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H2 Claim Kings and Combine Men
square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented office note, claim warning, or clean-cuff saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Owners, Brokers, and Quiet Thieves
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing mine owners, syndicate agents, brokers, and manipulators who turn land, debt, and law into control
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate claim kings and combine men as upper-pressure antagonists, uneasy patrons, and paper owners whose reach is felt before they enter a room.
Tie future moves to paper ownership, bought testimony, payroll squeeze, proxy violence, courthouse favors, public charity with teeth, and a private road that was public yesterday.
Avoid mustache-twirling; make their manners better than their morals.
Let the page show how money can hire guards, stores, law, rumor, road crews, and even mercy.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Their villainy should arrive on clean cuffs and leave in a private carriage. The richest man in town may never touch a shovel, but every shovel knows his name.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A claim-king scene should ask which paper matters, which witness was bought, and what kindness is about to become a chain.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western claim, office, and courthouse language only.
Keep ownership pressure practical, social, and morally dangerous.
The generated page must include image path images/claim-kings-and-combine-men.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make wealth cartoonish or cleanly evil; it should be useful enough to tempt people
do not use modern corporate language, modern office gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about ownership, debt, claims, courthouse leverage, proxy force, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
